title: Audio Specification for Rendered Musical-Grammar Demonstration
description: Schema the LLM emits as JSON in section 9. The synthesis engine reads this and renders a 30–90 s demonstration. Every field must be consistent with the grammar described in sections 2–6 of the field notes.

encoding.description: How the pitches array is interpreted. 'ratio' = frequency ratios relative to reference_hz. 'cents_above_reference' = cents above the reference. 'hz_absolute' = explicit Hz values (reference_hz then ignored).

reference_hz.description: The reference pitch (1/1 or 0 cents). Typically the drone or tonic. Irrelevant if encoding=hz_absolute.

pitches.description: The pitches of the system. Must not include the octave-repetition — that is inferred from octave_repeats. Example (ratio): [1, 9/8, 5/4, 4/3, 3/2, 5/3, 15/8]

octave_repeats.description: If true, pitches are assumed to repeat at 2:1 above and below. If false, the system is non-octave-repeating (must match scale_topology in the field notes).

inflection_rules.description: Optional. Rules for microtonal inflection applied to specific pitches at performance time. Example: [{pitch_index: 2, inflection_cents_range: [-30, 0], direction: 'down'}] for a pitch bent down by up to 30 cents.

accent_pattern.description: One value per beat, 0.0-1.0, relative strength.

phrase_durations_seconds.description: Explicit list of phrase durations. Each phrase is a unit of performance.

cue_points_seconds.description: Times at which a ritual cue (e.g. bell, shout, gesture) advances the form.

notation_note.description: Free-text description of the proportional timing rule.

timbre.description: Short descriptor. Synth engine will map to its available voices. Examples: 'sine', 'vocal_male_low', 'bowed_string', 'struck_metal', 'reed', 'breath_noise', 'plucked_string', 'overtone_whistle'.

pitch_indices.description: Indices into pitch_system.pitches. The voice draws from these pitches.

rhythm_role.description: How the voice relates to the rhythm system.

spatial_position.description: Optional [x, y] in [-1, 1] stereo/quad space. Default [0, 0]. Used for spatially_distributed ensembles.

active_sections.description: Optional. Section names in form.sections during which this voice is active. Default: all sections.

arc_type.description: Overall form. Must match formal_type in field notes.

character.description: One-line descriptor of this section's character. E.g. 'sparse, single voice' or 'full ensemble, accelerating'.

rule.description: How and when ornaments are applied. E.g. 'Every sustained pitch longer than 2 s receives a descending meend of 20–50 cents.'

events.description: Optional explicit event list. If provided, overrides the rhythm_system + voices interaction for parts of the piece that need precise scripting. Prefer generative parameters where possible — only use events when the grammar explicitly requires it.

ornament.description: Optional. Named ornament to apply — must match something defined in ornamentation.rule.
